title: PolarQuant DOA Gate-B, held-out LOCATA confirmation
calibration: dev/task1 recordings {1,2,3} (config selection only, via structural-fuzzing)
heldout: eval/task1 DICIT recordings (13, single static loudspeaker), unseen at seal time

support_budget64.note: not the sealed bar; shown for completeness, does not rescue the miss

interpretation.robust_positive: Destroying the phase/angle catastrophically destroys DOA on 13/13 held-out recordings (anti worst). The directional information lives in the angle.
interpretation.registered_negative: The subtle consumer-relative flip (angle-preserving PolarQuant BEATS reconstruction-optimal scalar on DOA at matched bits, a clean two-halves flip) does NOT robustly transfer to real held-out LOCATA DOA (6/13 < 8/13). Parity, not a win; the calibration 74% was over-optimistic selection.
interpretation.bearing: GO-P-2026-031 (simulation) stands on its own; its real-data transfer of the SUBTLE effect is an honest negative. The coarse angle-carries-direction claim holds on real unseen data.
